data.overview: VibeCode API proxies Bitrix24 REST API. 39 entities across CRM (deals, contacts, companies, leads, activities, products, product-rows, quotes, invoices, old-invoices, requisites, smart-processes, catalog-sections, catalog-prices, payments, basket-items, order-statuses), tasks, users, calendar, disk, chat, document generator, booking, bizproc, open lines, telephony, and more. No /v1/call needed — all methods have dedicated wrappers. Two levels: entity API (recommended) and batch. Plus action APIs for telephony, CRM triggers, timeline logs, feed posts, warehouses, workflows, notifications, and task time. Bot API for chat bots (imbot.v2), workday tracking (timeman), and Infrastructure API for deploying applications to cloud servers. NOTE: /v1/invoices wraps new smart invoices (crm.item.*). For legacy CRM invoices (crm.invoice.*) use /v1/old-invoices.

params.filter: object (optional) — filter conditions (MongoDB, prefix, or operator style)
params.pageSize: number (optional, default 50) — records per page, auto-paginates if > 50
params.autoWindow: boolean (optional, default true) — set to false to disable date-based windowing for large datasets

windowing.description: For large datasets, search auto-splits queries into date windows for reliability. Disable with autoWindow: false if windowing causes timeouts.
windowing.meta: Response meta includes windowCount (number of date windows used) and windowErrors (count of failed windows) when windowing is active.
windowing.errorHandling: Per-window errors are non-fatal — failed windows are skipped and logged, remaining windows still return results.

params.op: required — count, sum, avg, min, max
params.field: required for sum/avg/min/max — one of the aggregatable fields
params.groupBy: optional — field to group results by

botApi.description: Bot platform (imbot.v2) — managed bot lifecycle. Register bots, poll events, send messages, manage chats, slash-commands, and files.
important: ALWAYS use VibeCode Bot API endpoints (/v1/bots/*) for ALL bot operations. Bitrix24 REST methods (imbot.register, imbot.message.add, im.message.get, etc.) have no direct endpoint — use /v1/bots/* wrappers.
VibeCode manages botToken automatically — you never see or handle it. Just use your API key (X-Api-Key header) and botId.
All requests go to https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots/... with X-Api-Key header. No Bitrix24 portal URL needed.

body.name: string (required, display name shown in chat)
body.type: string (bot | personal | supervisor | openline, default: bot). Type cannot be changed after registration.
body.backgroundId: string (optional, chat background). Values: azure, mint, steel, slate, teal, cornflower, sky, peach, frost. Invalid values become null.

registration.note: Vibe maps top-level name/lastName/workPosition/color/gender/avatar into fields.properties.{field} for Bitrix24 automatically.
registration.response: { bot: { id, code, type, eventMode, isHidden, isReactionsEnabled, ... }, users: [{ id, name, firstName, lastName, ... }] }
registration.responseNote: Response returns full bot object at data.bot (access bot ID via data.bot.id). botToken is auto-generated and stored securely — you never see it.

completeExample.title: Minimal working bot — register, poll, respond

steps.code: const res = await fetch("https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots", { method: "POST", headers: { "X-Api-Key": API_KEY, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ code: "my_bot", name: "My AI Bot" }) }); const { data } = await res.json(); const botId = data.bot.id;

const eventsRes = await fetch("https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots/" + botId + "/events?limit=50", { headers: { "X-Api-Key": API_KEY } }); const { data: events } = await eventsRes.json(); // offset is tracked server-side, no need to manage it

for (const event of events) { if (event.type === "ONIMBOTV2MESSAGEADD") { const dialogId = event.data.chat.dialogId; await fetch("https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots/" + botId + "/typing", { method: "POST", headers: { "X-Api-Key": API_KEY, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ dialogId }) }); await fetch("https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots/" + botId + "/messages", { method: "POST", headers: { "X-Api-Key": API_KEY, "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ dialogId, fields: { message: "Hello! I received your message." } }) }); } }

endpoints.description: Get API revision numbers for compatibility checking. No botId required.

pollingExample.description: Typical event loop: poll → process → respond. Server tracks offset — omit offset param to auto-resume from last position.
steps: GET https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots/:botId/events?limit=100 (no offset = server uses stored lastOffset, safe across restarts)
Process events array: event.type === "ONIMBOTV2MESSAGEADD" means new message. Event data contains bot, message, chat, user objects. Use chat.dialogId for dialog identification.
POST https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots/:botId/typing {dialogId, statusMessageCode: "IMBOT_AGENT_ACTION_THINKING"}
POST https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots/:botId/messages {dialogId, fields: {message: "Response text"}}
Repeat — server auto-advances offset after each poll. Pass offset=0 only to replay all history.

offsetBehavior.noOffset: Uses server-stored lastOffset (survives bot restarts, no duplicate processing)
offsetBehavior.offsetN: Starts from specific event ID (inclusive — the event with that ID is included in response). To avoid duplicates, always use nextOffset from previous response.
offsetBehavior.responseFields: Response: { events[], nextOffset (pass as offset in next request), hasMore (boolean), storedOffset (current DB value) }

keyboardFields.description: Full keyboard button fields — all passed through to Bitrix24. 17 supported fields per button.
keyboardFields.important: keyboard MUST be a FLAT array of button objects, NOT nested arrays. Use DISPLAY: "LINE" to start a new row.

attachFormat.description: Message attachments — rich cards below the message text.
attachFormat.important: attach MUST be a flat array of block objects, NOT nested in BLOCKS.

dialogIdFormat.description: dialogId format for sending messages, typing, files.
dialogIdFormat.dm: For direct messages: dialogId = userId as string. Example: "1" for user 1, "192" for user 192.
dialogIdFormat.groupChat: For group chats: dialogId = "chat{chatId}". Example: "chat123" for group chat with chatId=123.
dialogIdFormat.important: Arbitrary strings like "chat_support_1" will cause CHAT_NOT_FOUND error. Only numeric userIds or "chat{N}" format.

fileUpload.description: Upload files to chat via POST /v1/bots/:botId/files.
fileUpload.format: { dialogId: "1", fields: { name: "report.pdf", content: "base64...", message: "optional text" } }
fileUpload.important: Fields are name + content inside "fields" object (NOT inside "file" sub-object). content is base64-encoded. Max 100 MB.

stickers.description: Stickers are NOT supported in Bot API v2. Bots can send: text messages, files, keyboard buttons, and attach cards.
stickers.note: Users can send stickers TO bots — they arrive as events. But bots cannot send stickers back.

botTypes.bot: Standard bot — responds to @mention and direct messages. Most common type. Cannot use Message.get or Message.getContext.
botTypes.personal: AI assistant — receives ALL messages in chats without @mention. Can use Message.get and Message.getContext endpoints. Hidden from search for some user groups.
botTypes.supervisor: System observer — receives ALL messages in chats where it participates. Can use Message.get and Message.getContext endpoints. For monitoring use cases.
botTypes.openline: Open Lines bot — for customer service channels via Open Lines. Requires isSupportOpenline: true at registration.
botTypes.note: Bot type cannot be changed after registration (Bot.update does not accept type). Only personal and supervisor bots can use Message.get (EP-11) and Message.getContext (EP-12).

ONIMBOTV2MESSAGEADD.data: { bot: {id, code, type, isHidden, eventMode}, message: {id, chatId, authorId, date, text, isSystem, uuid, forward, params, viewedByOthers}, chat: {id, dialogId, type, name, entityType, owner, avatar, color}, user: {id, active, name, firstName, lastName, workPosition, color, avatar, gender, birthday, extranet, bot, connector, externalAuthId, status, idle, lastActivityDate, absent, departments, phones, type}, language }

ONIMBOTV2MESSAGEUPDATE.data: { bot, message, chat, user, language } — same structure as MESSAGEADD

ONIMBOTV2MESSAGEDELETE.data: { bot, MESSAGE_ID (number, NOT full message object), chat, user }

ONIMBOTV2COMMANDADD.data: { bot, message, chat, user, command: {id, command, params, context} } — context: "textarea" | "keyboard" | "menu"

ONIMBOTV2REACTIONCHANGE.data: { bot, reaction (string), action ("add"|"delete"), message, chat, user }

ONIMBOTV2DELETE.data: { bot } — only bot object, no chat/user/dialogId

ONIMBOTV2CONTEXTGET.data: { bot, context: {entityId, entityType, source}, chat, user }

userScopeEventTypes.description: User-scope events (ONIMV2*) — require im.v2.Event.subscribe (im scope, not imbot) + withUserEvents=true in polling. Same data structure as bot events but triggered by user actions in subscribed chats.

fetchVsWebhook.fetch: Native types (int, bool, null). Full bot object. No auth field.
fetchVsWebhook.webhook: HTTP POST, Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded. All scalars as strings (http_build_query serialization). bot object includes auth with OAuth tokens. Each webhook is per-bot. Timeout: 10s. No automatic retry.

commonMistakes: DO NOT call imbot.register, imbot.message.add, im.message.get as raw methods — use /v1/bots/* endpoints instead.
DO NOT try to get or manage botToken yourself — VibeCode handles it automatically.
DO NOT use Bitrix24 portal URL for bot API calls — always use https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/bots/...
dialogId for 1-on-1 chats is the user ID (number). For group chats it starts with "chat" prefix (e.g. "chat123").

aiApi.description: AI Model Router — OpenAI-совместимый API для работы с AI моделями. Бесплатная модель BitrixGPT 5 доступна по умолчанию.

endpoints.description: Список доступных моделей с ценами и характеристиками
Chat completion (синхронный и SSE streaming)

aiApi.autoMode: Если model не указан или "auto" — используется бесплатная модель BitrixGPT 5. Не требует оплаты.

models.openrouter: Через OpenRouter доступны Claude, GPT-4o и другие модели. Цены зависят от модели.
models.byok: Подключите свой API ключ от провайдера (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter) и используйте модели бесплатно через наш API.

streaming.description: Добавьте "stream": true в запрос для потокового ответа (Server-Sent Events)

examples.title: Простой запрос (бесплатная модель)
CRM-бот: классификация обращений
Генерация описаний товаров для CRM
Streaming: потоковый ответ

billing.description: Бесплатные модели (BitrixGPT 5, GPT-OSS 120B) не тарифицируются. Платные модели списывают стоимость в рублях с баланса аккаунта ежедневно. BYOK (свои ключи) — полностью бесплатно.

infraApi.description: Cloud infrastructure for deploying applications. Create virtual servers on Timeweb Cloud or Yandex Cloud, deploy your code via SSH or Vibe Black Hole. Two deployment modes: OPEN (direct SSH) and BLACKHOLE (pull-based secure tunnel with HTTPS subdomain).
infraApi.scope: No scope required — available to all API keys when infrastructure is configured on the platform.

endpoints.description: List available cloud providers with availability status
Get server plans (CPU, RAM, disk, price) for a provider
Get available datacenter regions
Get OS images (Ubuntu, Debian, etc.)
Create a new server. All servers are created in BLACKHOLE mode (secure tunnel with HTTPS subdomain). Body: { provider, name, plan, region, image, sshPublicKey? }. App must listen on port 3000.
List your servers (scoped to API key)
Get server details (auto-refreshes status from provider if provisioning)
Get SSH credentials. BLACKHOLE servers return null (use Deploy API). OPEN servers return direct IP+password. Check mode field.
Delete a server
Stop a running server
Start a stopped server
Update access policy for BLACKHOLE-mode servers (public/restricted/portal-only)
Switch server mode between BLACKHOLE and OPEN. Requires allowOpenMode portal policy. BH→OPEN opens firewall + generates SSH password. OPEN→BH restores firewall + clears SSH.

deploymentModes.BLACKHOLE: Default mode. Secure tunnel with HTTPS subdomain. Direct IP firewalled. Deploy via API (exec/upload/logs). App must listen on port 3000.
deploymentModes.OPEN: Optional mode (requires allowOpenMode policy). Adds direct SSH access via public IP. Switch via PATCH /v1/infra/servers/:id/mode.

steps: 1. Choose provider: GET https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/infra/providers
2. Pick plan, region, image: GET https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/infra/providers/:providerId/plans (and /regions, /images)
3. Create server: POST https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/infra/servers with { provider, name, plan, region, image, mode: "BLACKHOLE" }
4. Poll status: GET https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/infra/servers/:id — wait for status "running" AND blackholeStatus "connected"
5. Get SSH: GET https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/infra/servers/:id/ssh — for BLACKHOLE returns relay command (ssh -p 2222 subdomain@vibecode.bitrix24.tech)
6. SSH via relay, install deps, start app on port 3000
7. App live at https://<subdomain>.vibecode.bitrix24.tech
8. Set access: PATCH https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/v1/infra/servers/:id/access-policy { accessPolicy: "PORTAL" }

stageIdFormat.description: Deal stageId values use Bitrix24 category prefix format: C{categoryId}:{STAGE_CODE}.
stageIdFormat.details: Stage IDs like "C2:NEW", "C2:WON" mean category 2 (pipeline). Default pipeline (categoryId=0) uses bare codes: "NEW", "WON", "LOSE". Custom pipelines prefix with "C{N}:". To match stages reliably, check suffix.
stageIdFormat.commonMistake: Do NOT compare stageId === "WON" — it will miss "C2:WON". Always use .endsWith(":WON") || === "WON".

dealAmountFields.description: Deals have two amount fields: "amount" (final) and "opportunity" (expected). Both can be 0 or differ.
dealAmountFields.details: For WON deals, "amount" is the final closed amount. "opportunity" is the expected revenue set during pipeline stages. Either can be 0 if not filled in. For forecasting use "opportunity" (set earlier), for revenue reports use "amount" (set at close).

activityTypeIds.description: Activity typeId is a numeric field. Reference of known values:
activityTypeIds.tip: Filter calls: activities.filter(a => a.typeId === 2). Filter emails: a.typeId === 4. Some custom activities may use typeId=6.

calendarEventsRequiredParams.description: GET /v1/calendar-events REQUIRES type and ownerId query parameters. Without them the request will fail with 422.
calendarEventsRequiredParams.commonMistake: GET /v1/calendar-events without params returns 422. Always specify ?type=user&ownerId={userId}.

filesRequiredParams.description: GET /v1/files requires folderId query parameter (lists files in a folder). No global disk search — Bitrix24 API limitation.
filesRequiredParams.tip: To browse files: 1) GET /v1/storages → get rootFolderId, 2) GET /v1/folders?parentId={rootFolderId} → list children, 3) GET /v1/files?folderId={folderId} → list files in folder.

fieldCasing.description: All entity API responses use camelCase field names. The API automatically transforms Bitrix24 UPPER_CASE to camelCase. Workday and bot responses are also camelCase.

knownIssues.issue: Ignores >=CREATED filter (date filtering does not work)
knownIssues.workaround: Fetch all comments and filter by date on client side

knownIssues.issue: Does not support date filter. Pagination by 20 messages.
knownIssues.workaround: Paginate and filter by date on client side. Use LAST_ID for incremental reads.

knownIssues.issue: Maximum 50 records per request from Bitrix24 (applies to deals via crm.item.list with entityTypeId=2)
knownIssues.workaround: Use limit parameter in Entity API for auto-pagination, or /v1/batch for parallel pages

knownIssues.issue: ~40% of deals have no chat. Method returns error for such deals.
knownIssues.workaround: In batch calls handle errors per-element — mark deal as chat_id=NULL

knownIssues.issue: GET /v1/timelines requires entityType and entityId in filter. Without them returns empty results.
knownIssues.workaround: Always provide filter: POST /v1/timelines/search { filter: { entityType: "deal", entityId: 12345 } }

knownIssues.issue: Date filters (>=createdAt, >=updatedAt) break on wide ranges (>7 days). Bitrix24 iterates by ID ASC and applies filter per-page, returning hasMore:false before reaching newer records. Narrow ranges (1-3 days) work correctly.
knownIssues.workaround: Split wide date ranges into weekly windows: use $gte + $lt with 7-day intervals. Example: week1={$gte:"2026-03-01",$lt:"2026-03-08"}, week2={$gte:"2026-03-08",$lt:"2026-03-15"}, etc. Combine results client-side.

knownIssues.issue: SPA entities (deals, invoices) use camelCase fields internally. statusId does not exist — use stageId. UF fields appear as ufCrm_* (not UF_CRM_*).
knownIssues.workaround: Use stageId for filtering by stage. UF fields: use ufCrm_5A268AD7C9C17 format in select/filter for deals.

knownIssues.issue: !stageId (NOT-filter) may be ignored by Bitrix24 for some fields (deals, invoices via crm.item.list).
knownIssues.workaround: Use positive filters instead: { "stageId": ["WON", "NEW"] } or filter client-side.
Ensure webhook has "crm" scope. Recreate webhook on Bitrix24 portal with required permissions.

knownIssues.issue: GET /v1/leads returns empty results if leads are disabled in CRM settings (Simple Mode without leads). No error is returned.
knownIssues.workaround: Check portal CRM mode: if leads list is empty and deals work, leads are likely disabled. Use deals directly.

knownIssues.issue: Bitrix24 returns 422 "Document constructor must be public" on some portals. This is a server-side Bitrix24 bug.
knownIssues.workaround: Use static field definitions from GET /v1/documents/fields. The fields endpoint returns schema fields without calling Bitrix24.

knownIssues.issue: Requires mandatory iblockId parameter. Without it returns "Could not find value for parameter {filter}".
knownIssues.workaround: Pass iblockId query param: GET /v1/catalog-products/fields?iblockId=23. Without iblockId, only static schema fields are returned.

knownIssues.issue: Lists API (lists.element.*) may return "Method not found!" on portals without Universal Lists module enabled.
knownIssues.workaround: Verify that Universal Lists module is active on the portal. Entity endpoints for list-elements are currently disabled.

appOAuth.description: OAuth 2.0 flow for multi-user OAuth apps (app keys). Allows your app to act on behalf of individual Bitrix24 users. Each user authorizes once, then your app uses their session token for API calls.
appOAuth.builtInCallback: https://vibecode.bitrix24.tech/oauth/complete — built-in callback page. New apps get this as default redirect_uri. When redirect_uri is omitted, the platform handles the entire flow and delivers the token via polling.

platformCallback.description: Zero-config flow (recommended). Omit redirect_uri — the platform handles everything. Retrieve the token via polling.
steps: 1. Generate a random state (UUID, min 16 chars): state = crypto.randomUUID()
2. Open in popup or redirect: GET /v1/oauth/authorize?app_key=YOUR_KEY&state=STATE
3. User authorizes on Bitrix24 portal (one click)
4. Platform creates session automatically — user sees success page
5. Your app polls GET /v1/oauth/poll?app_key=YOUR_KEY&state=STATE until status=complete
6. Use access_token with Authorization: Bearer header for API calls

customRedirect.description: Traditional flow. Provide your own redirect_uri (must be registered in app settings).
steps: 1. Redirect user: GET /v1/oauth/authorize?app_key=YOUR_KEY&redirect_uri=YOUR_URL&state=STATE
2. User authorizes on Bitrix24 portal
3. User is redirected to YOUR_URL?code=ONE_TIME_CODE&state=STATE
4. Your backend exchanges code: POST /v1/oauth/token
5. Use session token with Authorization: Bearer header

authorize.description: Start OAuth flow. Redirect or open popup.

params.redirect_uri: string (optional) — omit to use platform callback. If provided, must match a registered URI.
params.state: string (required, min 16 chars) — CSRF protection. Use crypto.randomUUID().

poll.description: Poll for platform callback result. Returns pending until user authorizes, then returns access_token (single-use).

params.state: string (required, min 16 chars) — same state used in /authorize

poll.notes: Poll every 2 seconds. Result expires after 10 minutes. Single-use — consumed on first successful response.

token.description: Exchange one-time code for a session token. Only needed with custom redirect_uri. Call server-side.

body.redirect_uri: string (required) — must match the value used in /authorize

security.state_parameter: Required. Min 16 characters. Prevents CSRF attacks. Use crypto.randomUUID().
security.app_key_secret: Never expose in client-side code. Exchange code server-side only.
security.redirect_uri_validation: Custom URIs must match a registered URI. Configure in app edit page. Platform callback is always allowed.
security.poll_result: Single-use, expires in 10 minutes. Token is consumed on first successful poll response.

errorCodes.INVALID_REDIRECT_URI: redirect_uri not registered for this app. Add it in app edit page.
errorCodes.INVALID_CODE: Code is invalid, expired (>5min), or already used

partnerConnect.description: OAuth 2.0 flow for external services to obtain Vibe API keys on behalf of portal users. Allows third-party integrations (e.g. OpenClaw) to request scoped access without manual key sharing.

authorize.description: Start OAuth flow. Redirect the user to this URL. After approval, the user is redirected back to redirect_uri with a one-time code.

params.client_id: string (required) — your Partner app client ID
params.redirect_uri: string (required) — must match a pre-registered URI
params.scopes: string (required) — comma-separated list of requested scopes (e.g. "crm,task")
params.state: string (recommended) — opaque value to prevent CSRF, echoed back on redirect

token.description: Exchange the one-time code for a permanent API key. Call server-side only — never expose client_secret in browser.

params.code: string (required) — one-time code received at redirect_uri
params.redirect_uri: string (required) — must match the value used in /authorize

errorCodes.INVALID_CLIENT: Unknown or disabled client_id / client_secret mismatch
errorCodes.INVALID_REDIRECT_URI: redirect_uri not registered for this client
errorCodes.INVALID_SCOPE: One or more requested scopes are not allowed for this client
errorCodes.INVALID_CODE: Code is invalid, expired, or already used
errorCodes.PARTNER_DISABLED: Partner Connect is disabled for this portal
